channel.title: Hacking on Ivan Fraixedes' Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Hacking on Ivan Fraixedes' Blog

item.title: My 5 favourite tweets of the week #12
item.description:
Another “working” week is gone and as usual you can take a look here to my 5 favourite tweets of the week

I’ve got a nice post about Rust Iterators, don’t missed if you’ve started to be curious about this language that will be for production use in a few days after this post

A Journey into Iterators http://t.co/tuR0cIIG12

— Rust Language (@rustlang) May 2, 2015

Hacking is not my topic, however I’m always interested in news about it, so this tweet about Javascript-Based DDoS got my attention

An Introduction to JavaScript-Based DDoS: https://t.co/V6VU7agXOC

— JavaScript Daily (@JavaScriptDaily) May 3, 2015

The amazing community behind iojs has made a big step to move forward releasing 2.0 version, they deserved to add this tweet here

io.js 2.0 Released: https://t.co/hcF5CxV1yV (V8 upgraded to 4.8, npm to 2.9.0 & more, see https://t.co/flwmTNnJvP )

— JavaScript Daily (@JavaScriptDaily) May 5, 2015

The Linux distribution called Kali, has updated 3 packages related with Wireless Penetration Testing, so good news if you love to play around with wireless connections

Pixiewps, Reaver & Aircrack-ng - wireless penetration testing tool updates in Kali and what they mean for you - https://t.co/jgXn4t9nE1

— muts (@kalilinux) May 4, 2015

Dave Cheney has created a new build tool for Go, called gd which should solve the problem of a reproducible builds; one of thing that me and many other people miss in Go is an appropriated tool to manage dependencies so I’ll take a look to this totally different approach of others I’ve seen and tested, hence this tweet provided me this amazing discovery

Building Go projects with 'gb': https://t.co/f9igUiQOQP

— Go Newsletter (@golangweekly) May 8, 2015

Have a lovely weekend!
